title: Credentials and CEO maxims — early HS leadership rules
html:
Credentials and CEO maxims — early HS leadership rules

Source: 2019-08-15 Apple Note (HS-era, captured at age ~16). One of the earliest "rules-to-self" notes in the corpus, before FBLA activity peaked.

TL;DR

  • "The CEO's job isn't to make a decision, it is to make the right decision" — earliest founder-thinking maxim Yahir wrote down, predates the AGM startup advice note by 4 years.
  • "When faced with a problem, just ask other people" — proto version of the asking the right question concept.
  • "Don't specialize too early" — generalist credo that explains OSU business→CS pivot and the Shanghai study abroad decision.
  • "You have to be forced to be in a situation to learn from it... Chinese" — first written articulation of the voluntary adversity thesis, with Chinese class as the concrete instance.
  • "Follow up and don't leave a void" — operational discipline rule.

Why it matters

This 5-line note is the seed of three later concepts that recur for 6+ years: voluntary adversity, asking-the-right-question, and the right-decision (not a-decision) framing of leadership. Worth retroactive-linking from the Ahmad Jamal stream and AGM startup advice.

Connections

  • fbla — context (pre-college student leadership)
  • asking the right question
  • voluntary adversity
  • 2023 07 05 startup advice — later, denser version of similar maxims
  • — genre antecedent
